Name: competition_mechanics_Ch1_Q10
Header:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
Theorem:
theorem Ch1_Q10
  (v : Speed)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (t : Time)
  (r_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => v * t)
  (φ_ring : Time → Dimensionless
:=fun t => ω * t)
  (x_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.cos ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t)
  (y_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.sin ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t )
  (v_r : Time → Speed
:=physderiv r_ring)
  (v_φ : Time → Speed
:=fun t => r_ring t * ω)
  (a_r : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => ((physderiv v_r) t)  - ((r_ring t) * ω**2).cast (by module))
  (a_φ : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => 2 • v * ω)
  :
  (∀ t, r_ring t = (v / ω) * φ_ring t) ∧
  (∀ t, v_r t = v ∧ v_φ t = v * ω * t) ∧
  (∀ t, a_r t = -v * ω**2 * t ∧ a_φ t = 2 • v * ω) := by sorry
Statement:
import Mathlib
import Aesop
import Mathlib.Tactic
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SI
import PHYSlib.Foundations.SIDeriv
open BigOperators Real Nat Topology Filter Rat UnitsSystem SI
unseal Rat.add Rat.mul Rat.sub Rat.inv
theorem Ch1_Q10
  (v : Speed)
  (ω : Scalar (-time_unit))
  (t : Time)
  (r_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => v * t)
  (φ_ring : Time → Dimensionless
:=fun t => ω * t)
  (x_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.cos ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t)
  (y_ring : Time → Length
:=fun t => Real.sin ((φ_ring t).val) • r_ring t )
  (v_r : Time → Speed
:=physderiv r_ring)
  (v_φ : Time → Speed
:=fun t => r_ring t * ω)
  (a_r : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => ((physderiv v_r) t)  - ((r_ring t) * ω**2).cast (by module))
  (a_φ : Time → Acceleration
:=fun t => 2 • v * ω)
  :
  (∀ t, r_ring t = (v / ω) * φ_ring t) ∧
  (∀ t, v_r t = v ∧ v_φ t = v * ω * t) ∧
  (∀ t, a_r t = -v * ω**2 * t ∧ a_φ t = 2 • v * ω) := by sorry
Informal_statement: EXAMPLE Ch1_Q10 Q: A slender rod rotates uniformly with angular velocity ω around a fixed endpoint O in a plane. A small ring moves along the rod with constant speed v relative to the rod. At time t = 0, the ring is located at the endpoint O. Prove that the trajectory of the ring is an Archimedean spiral: r = (v / ω) φ where r is the distance from O and φ is the angle the rod makes with a fixed axis.
